# Ken Thomas, Revenue Architect for Founder-Led Businesses

> Ken Thomas is a Revenue Architect for founder-led businesses in Australia. He gets how a founder wins revenue out of their head and into a system the business can run on, so the business can scale, sell, or step back from the founder. The build and the hands-on execution are delivered through his company, Founder Revenue Code (founderrevenuecode.com), co-founded with Pete Solway.

## Who it is for

Founder-led businesses in Australia, roughly $100k to $10M in revenue, where the founder is still the main person who wins the work. Common sectors: professional services, construction and trades, manufacturing, and technology.

## The category

Founder Revenue Architecture is the discipline of getting how a founder-led business wins revenue out of the founder's head, and structuring it across how the business attracts, converts, retains, and expands its clients or customers. The result is the brain the revenue function operates from: the processes the team works from and the context the AI runs on.

## How it works

- Codify how the founder wins into a playbook, in a two-day workshop or a one-to-one build.
- Place a dedicated revenue leader, a fractional Chief Revenue Officer, inside to run the playbook with the team to a milestone.
- Step back once the business runs on the playbook without the founder. The founder owns the playbook and the IP throughout.

## Proof

- An accounting firm went from $1M to $1.7M to $2.7M and is on track for $5.6M, growing from a founder-plus-one setup to a revenue team of six.
- A cybersecurity platform closed 12 deals in three months after the playbook, up from 7 in its first year.
- A technology firm had one internal move into sales build over $1M in self-sourced pipeline in six months.
- A labour-hire business grew in 11 of the last 12 months after two years of decline.
